children.name: Batrachostomus
children.description: Smaller, more delicately built frogmouths with ornate, finely patterned plumage that often includes white spots, bars and frilled feather edges. They are found in tropical forests across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Batrachostomus afer
children.description: A frogmouth found in the forests of West and Central Africa.

children.name: Batrachostomus affinis
children.description: Blyth’s frogmouth is a species of bird in the family Podargidae. They are brownish or rufescent brown with a slightly round bill and tail, and have tufts of bristles in front of the eyes and at the base of the bill. Batrachostomus occur from India and Sri Lanka, across mainland southeast Asia and as far as Borneo, Java, and Sumatra.

children.name: Batrachostomus cornutus
children.description: The Sunda frogmouth is a medium-sized, old world species of bird within the order Podargiformes. It is also uncommonly referred to as the long-tailed frogmouth or the horned frogmouth. The term "Sunda" is based on the geographical range of the species to regions around the Sunda islands.

children.name: Batrachostomus javensis
children.description: The Javan frogmouth, sometimes known as Horsfield's frogmouth, is a species of bird in the family Podargidae. It is sometimes considered conspecific with the Blyth's and Palawan frogmouths. Found in Southeast Asia, Indonesia and the Philippines, it lives in subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest.

children.name: Batrachostomus microrhynchus
children.description: A frogmouth endemic to Sulawesi, Indonesia.

children.name: Batrachostomus mirus
children.description: A frogmouth endemic to the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Batrachostomus poliolophus
children.description: The Sumatran frogmouth, also known as the short-tailed frogmouth and the pale-faced frogmouth, is a nocturnal bird belonging to the family Podargidae. It is endemic to the island of Sumatra in Indonesia.

children.name: Batrachostomus proximus
children.description: A frogmouth endemic to Palawan in the Philippines.

children.name: Batrachostomus stellatus
children.description: Gould's frogmouth is a nocturnal species of bird in the order Caprimulgiformes and the family Podargidae. It is one of the 12 species in the Batrachostomus genus. Its common name commemorates the English ornithologist and bird artist John Gould (1804–1881).

children.name: Podargus
children.description: Large, robust frogmouths with broad, heavy bills and a cryptic grey-brown plumage that mimics broken branches. They are nocturnal, perch-hunting predators endemic to Australasia.

children.name: Rigidipenna
children.description: A monotypic genus of frogmouth endemic to the Solomon Islands.

children.name: Rigidipenna inexpectata
children.description: The Solomons frogmouth, also known as the Cinnamon frogmouth or Solomon Islands frogmouth, is a predatory bird in the frogmouth family. It was first described in 1901, but not recognized as a distinct species until 2007. The Solomons frogmouth is the only known member of the genus Rigidipenna.
